training_request_details.requested_class:
Requesting a two-day Mobile Training Unit visit to provide the following courses:

1. Patrol Tactics & Officer Safety Refresher (8 hours)
   - Emphasis on high-risk traffic stops, building approaches, contact/cover principles, and situational awareness for solo and two-officer units.
   - Incorporate recent case law updates relevant to South Carolina regarding use of force and vehicle searches.
   - Scenario-based practical exercises in and around patrol vehicles, including low-light conditions.

2. De-escalation and Communication Strategies (8 hours)
   - Focus on verbal de-escalation, crisis communication with individuals in mental health crisis, and techniques for slowing encounters when safe and feasible.
   - Incorporate role-play scenarios that reflect common calls in our jurisdiction (domestic disturbances, neighbor disputes, welfare checks, and mental health transports).
   - Include cultural competency and community trust-building elements.

3. Legal Update & Report Writing for Patrol Supervisors (4 hours – optional, if scheduling permits)
   - Recent South Carolina and federal case law impacting patrol operations, searches, seizures, and electronic evidence.
   - Best practices for supervisory review of incident reports, use-of-force documentation, and body-worn camera usage.

Preferred structure:
- Day 1: Patrol Tactics & Officer Safety (full 8-hour day)
- Day 2 (morning): De-escalation and Communication Strategies (4 hours)
- Day 2 (afternoon): Legal Update & Report Writing for Patrol Supervisors (4 hours)

Target audience:
- Primarily uniformed patrol deputies (1–10 years of service)
- Field Training Officers
- First-line supervisors (corporals and sergeants)

We are particularly interested in tailoring scenarios to:
- High-traffic corridors along I-20 and US-378 where we frequently encounter narcotics transport and impaired drivers.
- Residential neighborhoods where we have seen an uptick in mental-health-related calls and family disturbances.

If possible, we would appreciate the instructors reviewing a small selection of redacted reports and body-worn camera clips (to be provided in advance) to better align the practical exercises with our real-world issues.

training_site__logistics.location_name_and_address:
Lexington County Sheriff’s Department Training Center
521 Justice Way
Lexington, SC 29072
training_site__logistics.additional_site_information_parking_buil:
Parking:
- Ample free parking is available on-site in the main lot directly in front of the Training Center. There is overflow parking behind the building if needed; we will have temporary signage posted on training days to direct visiting officers.
- Marked patrol units can park along the east side of the building near the fenced training yard for easier access to vehicles used in scenarios.

Building Entrance & Check-In:
- All attendees should enter through the main Training Center entrance under the awning on the north side of the building. The doors will be unlocked starting at 07:30 each training day.
- A sign-in table will be set up immediately inside the lobby. Our training coordinator or designee will handle sign-in sheets, distribute name tags, and direct students to the classroom.
- Instructors can park in the small staff lot on the west side of the building and enter through the side staff entrance. We will have someone available by 07:00 to assist with equipment loading/unloading.

Security:
- This is a secured law enforcement facility. Sworn officers should have agency ID visible. Non-sworn attendees (if any) will be checked in at the front desk and issued temporary visitor badges.
- Firearms are permitted for sworn officers; however, any live-fire exercises are NOT planned for this training. We will designate a safe area and clear protocol if any weapon-handling demonstrations are necessary.

COVID / Health & Safety Protocols (current as of request date):
- Face masks are optional; attendees may wear them based on personal preference or agency policy.
- Hand sanitizer stations are available at the lobby entrance and inside the classroom.
- We will request that anyone who is actively ill (fever, flu-like symptoms, or testing positive for COVID-19) stay home and coordinate with their supervisor about make-up training.
- Seating will be arranged to allow reasonable spacing between participants where possible.

Breaks & Food:
- We have a break area with vending machines and a refrigerator/microwave that instructors may use.
- Coffee and water will be provided each morning by our agency.
- There are several nearby lunch options within a 10-minute drive (fast food and sit-down restaurants along US-378). We typically allow a 60-minute lunch break from 12:00–13:00 but can adjust to instructor preference.

Accessibility:
- The facility is ADA accessible, with ramp access at the main entrance and accessible restrooms located across from the classroom.

Other Notes:
- We can provide a separate small office or side room for instructors to store materials and prepare between sessions.
- If needed, we can coordinate access to our adjacent defensive tactics room and training yard for practical scenarios, weather permitting.
training_site__logistics.technical_equipment_other:
- Document camera (Elmo-style) connected to the classroom computer for displaying documents or small items.
- Portable PA system with wireless microphone available if the instructors prefer to use it during scenario debriefs or if we move outside.
- Whiteboard and multiple flip charts with markers for group exercises.
- Charging strip and extension cords available at the back of the classroom for laptops and other devices.
- Ability to play video from USB drive, DVD, or through secure internet streaming (subject to our IT firewall rules).
training_site__logistics.may_training_be_opened_for_other_agencie:
yes – We are open to having the training opened to surrounding agencies, with the following preferences:

1. Priority will be given to Lexington County Sheriff’s Department personnel, especially patrol deputies and first-line supervisors.
2. After our internal roster is finalized, we welcome attendees from neighboring agencies such as Cayce PD, West Columbia PD, Batesburg-Leesville PD, and South Carolina Highway Patrol troopers assigned to our region.
3. We would like to cap total attendance at or below our social-distancing capacity (approximately 28 participants) to ensure quality of instruction and safe spacing.
4. We ask that any outside agencies pre-register their attendees at least two weeks in advance so we can manage seating, parking, and materials.
5. We do not anticipate inviting civilian ride-alongs or non-sworn staff for this specific training block, as it is geared primarily toward sworn patrol operations.
